body.title: [sprint] core.hooksPath points to the dead agent_datacenter dir — repo git hooks silently disabled
body.intention: The repo's git hooks run from a path that exists, so any intended pre-commit/commit-msg enforcement actually fires instead of being silently skipped.
body.description:
`git config core.hooksPath` in .git/config is set to /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/.git/hooks — the pre-rename repo location (agent_datacenter → UnseenUniversity). That directory no longer exists, so git finds no hooks there and silently runs NONE — any pre-commit/commit-msg/pre-push enforcement the repo expects is disabled. Surfaced 2026-06-26 during launcher commits (a stray python3-venv/pip-install error printed once but did not reproduce on an empty commit; the confirmed, reproducible issue is the stale hooksPath). Fix options for Akien to choose: (a) unset core.hooksPath so git falls back to the live .git/hooks (correct if hooks should live in-repo); (b) repoint it to .../UnseenUniversity/.git/hooks; (c) if hooks were intentionally centralized elsewhere, point at that real path. Needs Akien's call on which hooks SHOULD run before flipping it.

**Affected files:** .git/config (core.hooksPath) — not a tracked file; a local git-config change, not a code edit.
**Design rules:** none apply (git config, no code/memory/persistence change).
**Scope boundary:** IN — correct/clear the stale core.hooksPath so hooks resolve to a real dir. OUT — authoring new hook content (separate work), other stale agent_datacenter references elsewhere in config/env (sweep them under their own ticket).
**Test plan:** After the fix: `git config --get core.hooksPath` resolves to an existing dir (or is unset); a probe commit runs the intended hooks (or cleanly runs none) with no path-not-found / spurious errors.
**Completion criteria:** core.hooksPath no longer points at a non-existent path; a test commit produces no stale-hook errors and the intended hook behavior (run real hooks, or none) is confirmed with Akien.
